description: Performance-evidence ledger for the broad imaging performance wave. Fill this from GLENDENNING medium/large run_workload.py results before claiming speedup fractions.

runs.correctness_status: Read-ahead disabled versus enabled product comparison is overall good; .image, .model, .psf, and .residual are bit-identical.
runs.notes: Final GLENDENNING medium standard-MFS clean pair after correcting max-live ownership. The producer acquires its memory permit before building a block; max_live_row_blocks=2 includes consumer-owned and producer-in-flight blocks. CASA was skipped for timing isolation.

runs.correctness_status: rust_only_skip_casa; benchmark products were not compared; targeted cubedata product-invariance test covers .psf/.residual/.image/.sumwt for read-ahead on this path
runs.notes: GLENDENNING medium standard cubedata dirty workload. Enabling read-ahead with the current memory planner was 22.1% slower because the extra live row-block reserve reduced active_planes from 60 to 40 and increased slab_count from 9 to 13. This is negative default evidence, not a speedup claim.

parallel_effective.baseline: mosaic shared source slab active_planes=8 worker_count=8
parallel_effective.candidate: memory formula expanded the requested 2-plane minimum to active_planes=8 worker_count=8; one ordinary shared-source slab

chanchunks_effective.candidate: 8 planes in 1 slab because the exact source-cache plus per-plane working set fits the memory target

runs.correctness_status: Product comparison is overall good: chanchunks=4 versus chanchunks=1 is bit-for-bit identical for .image, .residual, .psf, .sumwt, .weight, .pb, and .image.pbcor; CASA timing was skipped because imaging parameters and CASA semantics were unchanged
runs.notes: GLENDENNING medium seven-field mosaic dirty cube counterbalanced result. The old special path treated chanchunks=4 as an exact two-worker cap and regressed to 42.34s versus 27.29s. The replacement formula computes the largest active plane group from exact source-cache bytes, per-plane execution bytes, the memory target, and hardware worker capacity. Here it permits all 8 planes in one slab at an estimated 3.529GiB active set under a 16GiB target, so the unnecessary full-source-cache variant is bypassed. Across three ABBA/BAAB blocks the median paired wall is 8.92% lower, a 1.098x speedup, and every block is faster. Six runs per side span substantial host variance, so the counterbalanced paired result is the release claim. All seven products compare exactly.

runs.parallel_effective: fixed_tile grid_threads=4; dirty product finish selected metal-mpsgraph on Apple M4
runs.correctness_status: CASA run completed for the candidate timing artifact; no product comparison panel was generated; resident-product unit tests compare GPU product finish against CPU f32 finish
runs.notes: Historical GLENDENNING medium standard-MFS dirty workload. Corrected unsandboxed Metal evidence showed that sandboxed Swift/Metal checks could hide MTLCreateSystemDefaultDevice while the same host exposed Apple M4 Metal outside the sandbox. The then-current fixed 1250x1250x2 Auto guard prevented this measured end-to-end slowdown, but that dataset-shaped guard has since been removed. Current Auto FFT selection is based on input placement and backend capability, while GPU-resident accumulations remain on Metal and host accumulations remain on CPU. The historical dirty-product profile slice improved from 45.552ms to 30.544ms, a 1.49x speedup / 33.0% reduction, but end-to-end explicit Metal was 13.170s versus the 12.146s CPU wall baseline, 8.4% slower, with source/prepare/gridding variance dominating total wall time.

runs.parallel_effective: metadata-group compaction followed by disjoint 256x256 CPU tile workers writing two Metal-shared planes
runs.correctness_status: CPU versus Metal comparison is overall investigate for .image, .image.pbcor, and .residual because tiny f32 accumulation-order differences remain spatially coherent; .pb, .psf, .sumwt, and .weight are good. Maximum normalized RMS is 2.1691e-6.
runs.notes: Counterbalanced GLENDENNING large seven-field ALMA mosaic MFS dirty-product comparison passes the zero-tolerance no-slowdown gate. Its median paired block is 17.4% lower wall, but the three block deltas span -38.3% to +48.9% with 43.6 percentage-point IQR, so this row records no slowdown rather than a release speedup claim. The retained adjacent CPU/Metal artifacts prove backend identity and a 75.685ms to 26.616ms PSF-product reduction. The product comparison remains investigate for image, image.pbcor, and residual because small f32 accumulation-order differences are spatially coherent; PB, PSF, sumwt, and weight are good. Relative to the pre-compaction 57.179s CPU diagnostic, the shared grouped path remains 2.46x faster / 59.4% lower wall in the earlier controlled pair.

precision_policy.baseline: pre-probe metal row-run grouped + mosaic product finisher
precision_policy.candidate: fused mosaic MPSGraph export/crop/reduce/normalize command buffer

runs.parallel_effective: mosaic single-grid-owner with Metal row-run grouped accumulation
runs.correctness_status: Benchmark-only experiment was reverted after performance review; no product comparison was generated for the rejected candidate; focused MPSGraph, resident, and mosaic tests passed before the revert.
runs.notes: GLENDENNING large ALMA mosaic MFS dirty Metal diagnostic. A fused mosaic product command buffer was tested after the standard-product Metal fusion, but the product-finish slice regressed: profile psf_fft_ms changed from 67.930ms to 73.851ms, an 8.7% slowdown / 0.92x speedup. End-to-end wall was effectively flat but slightly worse, 20.252s to 20.276s. The probe was reverted and should not be counted as an accepted improvement. The sandboxed companion run reported metal_device_available=false and is retained only as a negative control for sandbox-hidden Metal.

runs.precision_policy: CASA-compatible f32 Briggs and Taylor weights with f64 mosaic grids
runs.parallel_effective: CPU sample-range full-grid with 4 grid threads; this sandboxed read-ahead pair did not expose Metal
runs.correctness_status: Read-ahead disabled versus enabled comparison is overall good. Maximum normalized RMS difference across Taylor image, residual, and PSF products is 2.9993e-11; image TT1 and residual TT1 are bit-identical.
runs.notes: Final GLENDENNING large seven-field ALMA mosaic MT-MFS bounded-memory pair with 6.06M active rows and 16 channels. Correctly bounded read-ahead improves wall time by 6.19%. The path remains compute-dominated: measured overlap is 4.384s and the producer spends 92.033s blocked behind the consumer, but the retained path is now a measured speedup rather than a neutral negative control.

precision_policy.baseline: f32 RustFFT product transform with pointwise host-f64 visibility grids
precision_policy.candidate: f64/Complex64 exact-plan Taylor moments narrowed into Metal-shared f32 tile grids

runs.parallel_effective: 4 CPU moment/tile workers, deterministic exact-plan records, 5 Metal-shared Taylor planes, Metal MPSGraph batch FFT
runs.correctness_status: Counterbalanced CPU-versus-Metal comparison is overall good for all seven Taylor products. Correlations are at least 0.99999999999984 and maximum normalized RMS is 8.5273e-7.
runs.notes: GLENDENNING large seven-field ALMA mosaic MT-MFS counterbalanced result: median paired block wall is 73.1% lower, a 3.71x speedup. The three block reductions are 69.7%, 73.1%, and 78.8% with 4.58 percentage-point IQR. Exact complete-plan grouping reduces accepted samples to about 7.4-7.8% as compact records; four workers write five Metal-shared Taylor planes with zero host full-grid, pack, or host-to-device grid-copy bytes. The retained CPU/Metal product pair is good for all seven Taylor products.

precision_policy.baseline: Metal-shared f32 Taylor grids with fixed scratch geometry
precision_policy.candidate: Metal-shared f32 Taylor grids with shape-derived scratch, tile, route-copy, and chunk formulas

runs.parallel_effective: 4 CPU moment/tile workers, tile edge 453, 9 disjoint tiles, 262144000-byte scratch budget, raw chunk limit 1219737
runs.correctness_status: Previous versus shape-derived Metal planner comparison is overall good for all seven Taylor products. Maximum normalized RMS is 9.1306e-7.
runs.notes: GLENDENNING large seven-field ALMA mosaic MT-MFS direct-Metal result. Replacing fixed scratch clamps, worker-tile assumptions, route-copy counts, and sample cutoffs with grid/support/term/worker/memory formulas reduced the retained single-run wall from 30.708s to 19.154s: 37.6% lower and 1.60x faster. This is an additional measured improvement on top of the counterbalanced 3.71x CPU-to-Metal result; it is recorded as a single-run implementation comparison, not a counterbalanced release claim.

runs.correctness_status: Both configurations execute exactly one source row block, so the requested depth-two candidate disables the producer queue and uses the same synchronous path as depth one.
runs.notes: Small negative-control row only, not a speedup claim. The counterbalanced median paired delta is -5.58%, with one noisy positive block and two negative blocks; the formal zero-tolerance no-slowdown verdict passes. Logs show enabled=false, queue_capacity=0, live_row_block_high_water=1, and row_blocks=1 for both configurations, proving that the general read-ahead path does not create a producer thread or queue when the source has only one block.

runs.precision_policy: CASA-compatible f32 Briggs and Taylor weights with direct Metal-shared f32 Taylor grids
runs.parallel_effective: CASA serial; casa-rs four-worker exact-plan compaction into Metal-shared Taylor grids
runs.correctness_status: Current-head CASA oracle is green for all primary products. Full comparison is overall investigate only for cancellation-scale PSF/residual TT1 and sumwt TT1; all image, model, PB, PBCOR, alpha, alpha-error, TT0, and TT2 products are good.
runs.notes: Current worktree runtime. Primary product normalized RMS is at most 3.7804e-7. Model TT0/TT1 and sumwt TT0/TT2 are exact; PB TT1 is zero in both implementations. PSF TT1 and residual TT1 absolute RMS differences are 6.99e-15 and 5.21e-12 on cancellation-scale reference products.

adjudication.rationale: The three investigate products are cancellation-scale Taylor terms. Their absolute errors remain bounded below 3e-6, and their normalized RMS remains below 3e-4; all non-cancellation image, model, PB, PBCOR, alpha, TT0, and TT2 products are good.

precision_policy.baseline: seven-worker CPU streaming W-projection with Complex32 output grids
precision_policy.candidate: Metal f32 partial grids with formula-derived fanout and deterministic host f64 cross-chunk reduction

parallel_effective.candidate: Metal W-projection for initial dirty and residual accumulation; CPU minor-cycle control

runs.correctness_status: Metal versus CASA image and residual normalized RMS are 5.3731e-5 with correlation 0.9999999986; PSF normalized RMS is 1.1137e-4 with correlation 0.9999999938 and good structure. Two independent Metal runs differ by at most 1.6696e-6 normalized RMS. Metal improves the CASA difference by 13.3x for image/residual and 11.8x for PSF relative to the CPU baseline.
runs.notes: GLENDENNING medium 3.09M-row, 64-channel W-projection counterbalanced comparison with 55 shape-derived W planes and six measured runs per side. All six Auto runs exposed Apple M4 Metal and selected the shared W Metal kernel for initial dirty and residual accumulation. The median paired ABBA/BAAB block is 17.94% faster, a 1.219x speedup; all six adjacent CPU/Metal pairs are faster. Aggregate medians are 15.515s CPU and 12.974s Metal. Cached and newly read blocks share the exact two-live-block stream, reaching high-water two with measured producer/consumer overlap. Production selection uses projected W, field geometry, update density, live Metal headroom, and memory geometry; it contains no dataset identity or fixed dataset-size thresholds.

runs.precision_policy: CASA and Metal f32 W-projection with identical imaging parameters and 55 independently auto-selected W planes
runs.parallel_effective: CASA WProjectFT; casa-rs Metal partial grids with deterministic host f64 cross-chunk reduction
runs.correctness_status: Fresh current-head CASA oracle is overall investigate, not bad. Image and residual normalized RMS are 5.3819e-5 with correlation 0.9999999986; PSF normalized RMS is 1.1140e-4 with correlation 0.9999999938 and good structure.
runs.notes: Fresh self-contained GLENDENNING medium oracle after W-plane formula correction, Metal-path consolidation, deterministic f64 cross-chunk reduction, and cached-block read-ahead integration. CASA and casa-rs independently select 55 W planes from the selected projected W range and field geometry. The timing ratio is recorded for completeness; the release performance claim remains the counterbalanced multi-CPU versus Metal record.

adjudication.rationale: Image and residual amplitude checks are good at 5.3819e-5 normalized RMS; PSF is 1.1140e-4 with good beam-scale structure and 7.6022e-6 maximum absolute error. Independent Metal runs differ by at most 1.6696e-6 normalized RMS, and the Metal result is substantially closer to CASA than the multi-CPU baseline.
